entries.rationale: T1 is the explicit no-new-policy/passive-change counterfactual, describing continuation of the existing municipal/state-maintained model with multigrade classrooms and ad hoc local negotiation absent a national framework, directly matching S1's status-quo-plus-incremental-adaptation description.

entries.direction_title: Structural grade reorganization (lower grades stay, upper grades centralize)
entries.rationale: T2 implements the Polish 1999-pattern structural reorganization explicitly described in S2: lower grades (1-4/1-6) remain village-based while upper grades or an ISCED2-type school are consolidated centrally, distinguishing structural reform from the 2017 reversal disruption.

entries.direction_title: Networked retention: clustering, association-run and multi-service hub models
entries.rationale: T3 directly implements S3's networked-retention family, combining French RPI-style inter-municipal clustering, Polish community-run/repurposing models, and multi-service hub conversion (school plus kindergarten/social/civic functions) as an alternative to both status quo and closure.

entries.direction_title: Consolidation with mitigation (closure as default, compensatory measures)
entries.rationale: T4 operationalizes S4's consolidation-with-mitigation model: closure as a managed default for genuinely non-viable/zero-enrollment villages, paired with statutory transport guarantees, TEIP-style targeted support, and transparent consultation/documentation rules to offset travel and equity harms.

entries.direction_title: National systemic framework: transparency, thresholds and evidence infrastructure
entries.rationale: T5 is the national systemic framework proposal establishing transparent minimum-viability thresholds, cost-visibility rules, teacher-training standardization, and a closure/outcome registry that informs rather than dictates local choices across T1-T4, matching S5 exactly.
